{{Short description|Men's association football team}}
{{good article}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Slovenia women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2025}}

| Coach              = [[Boštjan Cesar]]
| Captain            = [[Jan Oblak]]

| FIFA min date      = December 1993

| pattern_ra1        = _svn26h

| rightarm1          = FFFFFF
| shorts1            = FFFFFF

| pattern_la2        = _svn26a
| pattern_b2         = _svn26a
| pattern_ra2        = _svn26a

| pattern_so2        = 
| leftarm2           = 0044FF

| rightarm2          = 0044FF
| shorts2            = 0044FF
| socks2             = 0044FF
| First game         = Unofficial<br />{{flagicon|Kingdom of Yugoslavia}} '''Slovenia''' 0–5 {{fb-rt|France|1794}}<br />{{nowrap|([[Ljubljana]], [[Kingdom of Yugoslavia|Kingdom of SCS]]; 23 June 1921)}}<br />Official<br />{{Fb|Estonia}} 1–1 {{fb-rt|Slovenia}}<br />([[Tallinn]], Estonia; 3 June 1992)
| Largest win        = {{Fb|Oman}} 0–7 {{Fb-rt|Slovenia}}<br />([[Muscat]], Oman; 8 February 1999)
| Largest loss       = {{Fb|France|1974}} 5–0 {{Fb-rt|Slovenia}}<br />([[Saint-Denis, Seine-Saint-Denis|Saint-Denis]], France; 12 October 2002)
| World cup apps     = 2
| World cup first    = 2002
| World cup best     = Group stage ([[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]], [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]])
| Regional name      = [[UEFA European Championship|European Championship]]
| Regional cup apps  = 2
| Regional cup first = [[UEFA Euro 2000|2000]]
| Regional cup best  = Round of 16 ([[UEFA Euro 2024|2024]])
| website            = [https://www.nzs.si/reprezentanca/?id_menu=8 nzs.si]
}}
The '''Slovenia national football team''' ({{langx|sl|Slovenska nogometna reprezentanca}}) represents [[Slovenia]] in men's international [[Association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Football Association of Slovenia]], the governing body for [[football in Slovenia]]. The national squad is under the global jurisdiction of [[FIFA]] and is governed in Europe by [[UEFA]]. It competes in the three major professional tournaments available to European nations: the [[FIFA World Cup]], [[UEFA Nations League]] and the [[UEFA European Championship]]. Slovenia played its first official match in 1992, one year after the country gained [[Breakup of Yugoslavia|independence from Yugoslavia]]. The majority of Slovenia's home matches are played at [[Stožice Stadium]] in [[Ljubljana]].

Slovenia have qualified for a major tournament four times, twice for the FIFA World Cup and twice for the UEFA European Championship. They failed to progress from the group stage of these tournaments in their first three appearances, before finally doing so at [[UEFA Euro 2024]], when Slovenia qualified for the knockout stages by drawing all three group stage matches and being one of the best third-place teams. At the [[2010 FIFA World Cup]], Slovenia achieved their first (and so far only) victory in a major tournament, defeating [[Algeria national football team|Algeria]] 1–0. Other noteworthy results include a 1–0 win over [[Italy national football team|Italy]] in 2004, which was Italy's only defeat in the entire [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006 World Cup]] campaign (qualifiers and the final tournament).

[[Boštjan Cesar]] holds the record for most appearances for Slovenia, having played 101 times between 2003 and 2018. [[Zlatko Zahovič]] scored 35 goals for Slovenia and is the record holder for most goals scored.

==History==

===Origins and pre-independence years (1921–1991)===
Before Slovenia's independence in 1991, the national football team of Slovenia existed only as a regional team, not officially recognised by [[FIFA]]. On 23 June 1921, the Slovenian capital of [[Ljubljana]] hosted a match between [[France national football team|France]] and a selection of players from Slovenian clubs. One of the guests at the match was acting FIFA president [[Jules Rimet]],<ref name="firstmatch">{{cite news|url=https://snportal.si/zgodovina/prva-tekma-slovenske-reprezentance/|title=Prva tekma slovenske reprezentance|language=sl|access-date=11 June 2017|date=26 August 2015|author=T. D. L.|website=Slovenski nogometni portal}}</ref> who later initiated the first FIFA World Cup tournament. The French team won the match 5–0 and, although the match was not official by international standards, it was, at least in Slovenia, generally accepted as the first appearance of the Slovenia national team.<ref name="firstmatch"/><ref name="firstmatch2">{{cite news|url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/sportni-sos/prva-tekma-slovenske-nogometne-reprezentance-ze-leta-1921/315982|title=Prva tekma slovenske nogometne reprezentance že leta 1921|language=sl|access-date=11 June 2017|date=6 September 2013|first=Slavko|last=Jerič|publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]]}}</ref> In the following decades, Slovenia played several unofficial [[Exhibition game#Association football|friendly matches]], including in 1956 against [[China national football team|China]], in 1968 against [[Austria national football team|Austria]], in 1990 against [[Bosnia and Herzegovina national football team|Bosnia and Herzegovina]] and in 1991 against [[Croatia national football team|Croatia]].<ref name="firstmatch2"/>

===Independence, first victory and first qualifiers (1992–1998)===
In 1992, one year after Slovenia's independence from [[Yugoslavia]], the [[Football Association of Slovenia]] was admitted to FIFA.<ref>{{cite web |title=Developing football in Slovenia|url=https://www.uefa.com/nationalassociations/svn/|website=[[UEFA]] |access-date=24 March 2025}}</ref> In the same year, on 3 June, Slovenia played its first FIFA-recognised game, a friendly match against [[Estonia national football team|Estonia]] in [[Tallinn]].<ref name="nzshistory">{{cite web|title=Zgodovina|trans-title=History|url=http://www.nzs.si/Reprezentanca/Moske_reprezentance/A_reprezentanca/Zgodovina|publisher=[[Football Association of Slovenia]]|access-date=11 June 2017|language=sl}}</ref> The match ended in a 1–1 draw, with the first official goal for the team being scored by [[Igor Benedejčič]]. The first coach was [[Bojan Prašnikar]].<ref>{{cite news|first1=Rok|last1=Plestenjak|first2=Rok|last2=Viškovič|title=Estonija zgodovinska za Slovenijo in tudi Keka|url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/estonija-zgodovinska-za-slovenijo-in-tudi-keka-305356|publisher=[[Siol]]|date=11 October 2010|access-date=11 June 2017|language=sl}}</ref> On 7 April 1993, the two teams played another friendly, and this time Slovenia achieved its first international victory by winning the match 2–0.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Slovenia vs Estonia, 7 April 1993|url=https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=9896|website=eu-football.info|access-date=5 July 2021}}</ref>

In 1994, managed by [[Zdenko Verdenik]], Slovenia debuted in official competitions as the team competed in the [[UEFA Euro 1996 qualifying|UEFA Euro 1996 qualifiers]]. Slovenia played in Group 4 and finished in fifth place out of six teams, with eleven points.<ref>{{Cite web|title=European Championship 1996 qualifications|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/96e-q.html|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation|access-date=5 July 2021}}</ref> In the opening match of the qualifiers, Slovenia drew 1–1 at home against [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994 World Cup]] finalists [[Italy national football team|Italy]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Rijavec |first1=Matej |title=Video: Nepriznani gol Milaniča v Mariboru, v Vidmu dobro branil Zupan |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/video-nepriznani-gol-milanica-v-mariboru-v-vidmu-dobro-branil-zupan/253350 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]]|date=22 March 2011|access-date=5 July 2021|language=sl}}</ref> In [[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|1998 World Cup qualification]], Slovenia was drawn with [[Denmark national football team|Denmark]], Croatia, [[Greece national football team|Greece]] and Bosnia and Herzegovina, finishing in last place after registering just one point in eight matches.<ref>{{Cite web|title=World Cup 1998 Qualifying|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/98q.html|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation|access-date=5 July 2021}}</ref>

===Golden generation (1998–2002)===

====UEFA Euro 2000 campaign====
In July 1998, [[Srečko Katanec]] was appointed as the new manager of the team.<ref>{{cite web |title=2. julij: Katanec prvič sedel na klop reprezentance |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/moja-generacija/2-julij-katanec-prvic-sedel-na-klop-reprezentance/331238 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]]|date=2 July 2018|access-date=5 July 2021|language=sl}}</ref> Under his guidance, Slovenia finished the [[UEFA Euro 2000 qualifying|UEFA Euro 2000 qualifiers]] in second place, only behind [[Norway national football team|Norway]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=European Championship 2000|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/00e.html|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation |access-date=5 July 2021}}</ref> [[Zlatko Zahovič]] scored eight of Slovenia's twelve goals in the qualifiers.<ref>{{cite web|first=Aleš|last=Golob|title=Zahovič v Atenah spisal prvo poglavje nogometne pravljice|url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/zahovic-v-atenah-spisal-prvo-poglavje-nogometne-pravljice/521303 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]]|date=23 April 2020|access-date=5 July 2021|language=sl}}</ref> In the [[UEFA Euro 2000 qualifying play-offs|additional play-offs]], Slovenia faced [[Ukraine national football team|Ukraine]]. The first leg was played in Ljubljana, which Slovenia won 2–1 after trailing 1–0. Zahovič scored Slovenia's first goal, and [[Milenko Ačimovič]] scored a goal from the halfway line late in the match to make the score 2–1.<ref>{{cite news|first=Matej|last=Rijavec|title=Video: 10 let od Ačimovičevega gola s sredine igrišča|url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/video-10-let-od-acimovicevega-gola-s-sredine-igrisca/216771|publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]]|date=13 November 2009|access-date=11 June 2017|language=sl}}</ref> The second match was played in snowy conditions in [[Kyiv]]. [[Serhii Rebrov]] scored from the [[Penalty kick (association football)|penalty spot]] in the 68th minute to give Ukraine a lead, while Slovenia equalised eight minutes later with a goal by [[Miran Pavlin]].<ref name="euro2000-siol">{{cite news|first=Rok|last=Plestenjak|title=Premraženi Slovenci kljub "svinjskemu" sojenju šokirali svet|url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/skok-v-sportno-preteklost-kijev-1999-512025|publisher=[[Siol]]|date=17 November 2019|access-date=11 June 2017|language=sl}}</ref> The 1–1 draw meant that Slovenia won 3–2 on aggregate and qualified for its first major tournament.<ref name="euro2000-siol"/>

{{football squad on pitch|align=right
|  GK|  GK = [[Mladen Dabanovič|'''Dabanovič''']]
| SW| SW = [[Željko Milinovič|'''Milinovič''']]
| RCB| RCB = [[Marinko Galič|'''Galič''']]
| LCB| LCB = [[Darko Milanič|'''Milanič''']]
| RWB| RWB = [[Džoni Novak|'''Novak''']]
| LWB| LWB = [[Amir Karić|'''Karić''']]
|  DM|  DM = [[Aleš Čeh|'''Čeh''']]
| RCM| RCM = [[Miran Pavlin|'''Pavlin''']]
| LCM| LCM = [[Zlatko Zahovič|'''Zahovič''']]
| RCF| RCF = [[Sašo Udovič|'''Udovič''']]
| LCF| LCF = [[Mladen Rudonja|'''Rudonja''']]
| caption = Slovenia's lineup for their first ever game at a major tournament, against [[Serbia and Montenegro national football team|FR Yugoslavia]] at [[UEFA Euro 2000]].<ref name="slo-yug33">{{cite web |title=History: Yugoslavia 3–3 Slovenia {{!}} UEFA Euro 2000 |url=https://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/match/64857--yugoslavia-vs-slovenia/ |publisher=[[UEFA]] |access-date=5 December 2019}}</ref>
}}
At Euro 2000, Slovenia were drawn into Group C, together with [[Spain national football team|Spain]], [[Serbia and Montenegro national football team|FR Yugoslavia]] and Norway. In the first game, Slovenia played against Yugoslavia and took a 3–0 lead after one hour of play, with Zahovič scoring twice and Pavlin once. However, Yugoslavia made a comeback as they scored three goals in just six minutes for a 3–3 draw, despite playing with only ten players after [[Siniša Mihajlović]] was sent off.<ref name="slo-yug33"/> The second game against Spain was played in [[Amsterdam]]. Spain took a 1–0 lead with a goal by [[Raúl González|Raúl]], but Slovenia equalised after one hour of play as Zahovič scored his third goal of the tournament. Just a minute later, Spain were leading again as [[Joseba Etxeberria]] scored the winning goal.<ref name="slo-spain2000">{{Cite web|title=History: Slovenia 1–2 Spain {{!}} UEFA Euro 2000|url=https://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/match/64860--slovenia-vs-spain/|publisher=[[UEFA]]|access-date=5 July 2021}}</ref> In the last match of the group stage, Slovenia played against Norway and still had a chance to progress to the quarterfinals. The match finished in a goalless draw and Slovenia won its second point of the tournament, but were eliminated.<ref>{{Cite web|title=UEFA Euro 2000 {{!}} Standings|url=https://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/history/seasons/2000/standings/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200812210529/https://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/history/seasons/2000/standings/|url-status=dead|archive-date=12 August 2020|publisher=[[UEFA]]|access-date=5 July 2021}}</ref>
{{#invoke:Sports table|main|style=WDL
|team1=ESP
|team2=YUG
|team3=NOR
|team4=SVN
|result1=KO
|result2=KO
|win_ESP=2 |draw_ESP=0 |loss_ESP=1 |gf_ESP=6 |ga_ESP=5
|win_YUG=1 |draw_YUG=1 |loss_YUG=1 |gf_YUG=7 |ga_YUG=7
|win_NOR=1 |draw_NOR=1 |loss_NOR=1 |gf_NOR=1 |ga_NOR=1
|win_SVN=0 |draw_SVN=2 |loss_SVN=1 |gf_SVN=4 |ga_SVN=5
|name_ESP={{fb|ESP}}
|name_YUG={{fb|FR Yugoslavia}}
|name_NOR={{fb|NOR}}
|name_SVN={{flagdeco|Slovenia}} '''Slovenia'''
|col_KO=green1 |text_KO=Advance to [[UEFA Euro 2000#Knockout stage|knockout stage]]
|update=complete
|source=[https://www.rsssf.org/tables/00e-final.html RSSSF]
}}

====2002 FIFA World Cup campaign====
For the [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification|2002 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]], Slovenia were drawn into a group with [[Russia national football team|Russia]], FR Yugoslavia, [[Switzerland national football team|Switzerland]], [[Faroe Islands national football team|Faroe Islands]] and [[Luxembourg national football team|Luxembourg]].<ref name="rsssf-2002">{{cite web |title=World Cup 2002 Qualifying|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/2002q.html|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation |access-date=7 July 2021}}</ref> In its first five qualifying games, Slovenia won only one match (away at Luxembourg). Underdogs Faroe Islands held them to a 2–2 draw, after the Slovenes were leading 2–0 with less than five minutes remaining.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Viškovič |first1=Rok |title=Fersko opozorilo iz leta 2000 |url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/fersko-opozorilo-iz-leta-2000-23338 |publisher=[[Siol]] |access-date=7 July 2021 |language=sl |date=31 May 2011}}</ref> In the next five matches, Slovenia won four, including a last minute victory over Russia.<ref name="rsssf-2002"/> The team finished in second place without a single defeat and advanced to the play-off stage, where they faced [[Romania national football team|Romania]].<ref name="rsssf-2002"/> Slovenia came from a goal down to win the first game 2–1 with the goals from Ačimovič in the first half and [[Milan Osterc]] in the second half.<ref name="rsssf-2002"/> In the second leg in [[Bucharest]], Slovenia took the lead with a goal scored by [[Mladen Rudonja]].<ref name="rsssf-2002"/> The final result was 1–1, and Slovenia qualified for its second consecutive major tournament and first ever World Cup.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/world_cup_2002/1658380.stm|title=Slovenia in wonderland|date=15 November 2001|access-date=11 June 2017|publisher=[[BBC Sport]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/video-10-let-od-prve-uvrstitve-slovenije-na-nogometni-sp/270529|title=Video: 10 let od prve uvrstitve Slovenije na nogometni SP|date=14 November 2011|access-date=11 June 2017|language=sl|publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]]|first=Matej|last=Rijavec}}</ref>

At the World Cup, Slovenia played in Group B with Spain, [[Paraguay national football team|Paraguay]] and [[South Africa national football team|South Africa]]. In the first game, Slovenia faced Spain in a Euro 2000 rematch. Spain took the lead in the first half with a goal from Raúl. [[Juan Carlos Valerón]] scored Spain's second goal with fifteen minutes remaining. A few minutes later, [[Sebastjan Cimirotič]] scored the first World Cup goal for Slovenia to cut the lead in half, before [[Fernando Hierro]] scored in the 87th minute for a 3–1 Spanish victory.<ref>{{cite web |title=Spain 3–1 Slovenia |url=https://www.fifa.com/tournaments/mens/worldcup/2002korea-japan/match-center/43950008 |publisher=[[FIFA]] |access-date=7 July 2021}}</ref> The match is notable due to the conflict between manager Katanec and star player Zahovič, after which Katanec announced his retirement after the tournament, while Zahovič was expelled from the team and sent home.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Plestenjak |first1=Rok |title=Zlatko Zahović o sporu v Južni Koreji: Rudi Zavrl se je odločil, da bo konflikt |url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/zlatko-zahovic-koreja-2002-526881 |publisher=[[Siol]] |access-date=7 July 2021 |language=sl |date=3 June 2020}}</ref> Slovenia lost the two remaining matches against South Africa (1–0) and Paraguay (3–1) and finished last in the group with three defeats.<ref>{{cite web |title=South Africa 1–0 Slovenia |url=https://www.fifa.com/tournaments/mens/worldcup/2002korea-japan/match-center/43950024 |publisher=[[FIFA]] |access-date=7 July 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Slovenia 1–3 Paraguay |url=https://www.fifa.com/tournaments/mens/worldcup/2002korea-japan/match-center/43950040 |publisher=[[FIFA]] |access-date=7 July 2021}}</ref>

{{#invoke:Sports table|main|style=WDL
|team1=ESP
|team2=PAR
|team3=RSA
|team4=SVN
|result1=KO
|result2=KO
|win_ESP=3 |draw_ESP=0 |loss_ESP=0 |gf_ESP=9 |ga_ESP=4
|win_PAR=1 |draw_PAR=1 |loss_PAR=1 |gf_PAR=6 |ga_PAR=6
|win_RSA=1 |draw_RSA=1 |loss_RSA=1 |gf_RSA=5 |ga_RSA=5
|win_SVN=0 |draw_SVN=0 |loss_SVN=3 |gf_SVN=2 |ga_SVN=7
|name_ESP={{fb|ESP}}
|name_PAR={{fb|PAR|1990}}
|name_RSA={{fb|RSA}}
|name_SVN={{flagdeco|Slovenia}} '''Slovenia'''
|col_KO=green1 |text_KO=Advance to [[2002 FIFA World Cup#Knockout stage|knockout stage]]
|update=complete
|source=[https://www.rsssf.org/tables/2002full.html RSSSF]
}}

===Decline (2003–2007)===
After the resignation of Katanec, Prašnikar took over the team on a four-year contract.<ref>{{cite web |title=Prašnikar bo podpisal za štiri leta |url=https://sta.si/658339/prasnikar-bo-podpisal-za-stiri-leta |publisher=[[Slovenian Press Agency]] |access-date=7 July 2021 |language=sl |date=8 July 2002}}</ref> The team underwent major changes, with several key players retiring from the national team.<ref name="derby1">{{cite web |last1=Plestenjak |first1=Rok |title=Hrvaška zaušnica, ki je zapekla Slovenijo kot še nobena |url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/skok-v-sportno-preteklost-slovenija-hrvaska-2003-541761 |publisher=[[Siol]] |access-date=7 July 2021 |language=sl |date=20 December 2020}}</ref>

In the [[UEFA Euro 2004 qualifying|Euro 2004 qualifying]] campaign, Slovenia played against France, [[Israel national football team|Israel]], [[Cyprus national football team|Cyprus]] and [[Malta national football team|Malta]]. The team finished in second place with four wins out of eight games, however, they lost both matches against France without scoring any goals.<ref name="2004rsssf">{{cite web |title=European Championship 2004|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/04e.html |publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation |access-date=7 July 2021}}</ref> In the play-offs, Slovenia played against its biggest rivals, Croatia. The first leg was played in [[Zagreb]], where Croatia took the lead as [[Dado Pršo]] scored a goal in the fifth minute, while Slovenia equalised in the 22nd minute with a goal by [[Ermin Šiljak]], for the final score of 1–1.<ref name="2004rsssf"/><ref name="siol-cro2004">{{cite web |last1=Plestenjak |first1=Rok |title=Bojan Prašnikar: Bunker proti Hrvaški? Takšne obsodbe me še danes zabolijo |url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/bojan-prasnikar-bunker-proti-hrvaski-taksne-obsodbe-me-se-danes-zabolijo-21434 |publisher=[[Siol]] |access-date=22 June 2021 |language=sl |date=18 November 2013}}</ref> In the second leg, Pršo scored the only goal of the game 15 minutes into the second half.<ref name="2004rsssf"/> Croatia qualified for Euro 2004 by winning 2–1 on aggregate, despite Šiljak scoring a total of nine goals in the whole campaign and becoming the top goalscorer of the whole Euro qualification tournament.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Rožman |first1=Andraž |title=Državni prvak v treh državah |url=https://www.dnevnik.si/204249 |website=[[Dnevnik (Slovenia)|Dnevnik]] |access-date=7 July 2021 |language=sl |date=3 October 2006}}</ref>

In May 2004, Prašnikar was replaced by [[Branko Oblak]].<ref name="oblak2004">{{cite news|url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/oblak-novi-selektor/102836|language=sl|date=24 May 2004|publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]]|access-date=11 June 2017|title=Oblak novi selektor}}</ref> Under Oblak's management, Slovenia played in the [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification|2006 World Cup qualifiers]]. Grouped with Italy, Norway, [[Scotland national football team|Scotland]], [[Belarus national football team|Belarus]] and [[Moldova national football team|Moldova]], the team started with victories over Moldova and Italy and a draw against Scotland, but still finished in fourth place after securing only five points in the remaining seven matches.<ref name="2006rsssf">{{cite web |title=World Cup 2006 Qualifying|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/2006q.html |publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation |access-date=7 July 2021}}</ref> With the victory over Italy, Slovenia became the only team to beat the [[2006 FIFA World Cup final|eventual world champions]], as Italy did not lose another game during the entire campaign (qualifiers and the final tournament).<ref>{{cite web |title=FOTO in VIDEO: Slovenija nad Belorusijo z dvojcem Iličić-Birsa, Cesar bo odigral prvih pet minut |url=https://www.24ur.com/sport/nogomet/foto-slovenija-nad-belorusijo-z-dvojcem-ilicic-birsa-cesar-bo-odigral-prvih-pet-minut.html?bl=1 |website=[[24ur.com]] |access-date=7 July 2021 |language=sl |date=26 March 2018}}</ref>

Grouped with the [[Netherlands national football team|Netherlands]], Romania, [[Bulgaria national football team|Bulgaria]], Belarus, [[Albania national football team|Albania]] and Luxembourg in [[UEFA Euro 2008 qualifying|Euro 2008 qualifying]], the team started out with defeats to Bulgaria and Belarus, thus significantly reducing their chances to qualify, and as a result, in November 2006, Oblak was dismissed by the Football Association of Slovenia.<ref name="oblak1106">{{cite news|url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/oblak-ni-vec-selektor-izbrane-vrste/115327|language=sl|date=27 November 2006|publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]]|access-date=11 June 2017|title=Oblak ni več selektor izbrane vrste}}</ref> During his two-year stint as the manager, Oblak used over forty different players.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.zurnal24.si/sport/nogomet/oblak-vedeli-smo-da-delamo-za-drugega-56825|language=sl|date=11 October 2009|website=[[Žurnal24]]|access-date=11 June 2017|title=Oblak: 'Vedeli smo, da delamo za drugega'}}</ref>

In January 2007, [[Matjaž Kek]] was appointed as the new manager of the national team.<ref name="kek2007">{{cite news|url=https://old.delo.si/sport/novi-selektor-je-matjaz-kek.html|language=sl|date=5 January 2007|website=[[Delo (Slovenia)|Delo]]|access-date=11 June 2017|title=Novi selektor je Matjaž Kek}}</ref> He led Slovenia in the remainder of the Euro 2008 qualifiers, where Slovenia finished in sixth place, only above Luxembourg.<ref name="2008rsssf">{{cite web |title=European Championship 2008|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/08e.html|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation |access-date=7 July 2021}}</ref>

===2010 World Cup campaign (2008–2011)===
[[File:Russia vs Slovenia World Cup 2010 Qualification, 2009-11-14 (48).jpg|thumb|left|Slovenian players celebrating [[Nejc Pečnik]]'s goal against Russia in the 2010 World Cup play-offs.]]
For the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification|2010 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]], Slovenia were drawn into a group with the [[Czech Republic national football team|Czech Republic]], [[Poland national football team|Poland]], [[Northern Ireland national football team|Northern Ireland]], [[Slovakia national football team|Slovakia]] and [[San Marino national football team|San Marino]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Slovencem slovanska skupina |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/slovencem-slovanska-skupina/120819 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=7 July 2021 |language=sl |date=25 November 2007}}</ref> In its opening match, Slovenia held Poland to a 1–1 draw in [[Wrocław]], before winning two consecutive home games against Slovakia and Northern Ireland.<ref name="2010rsssf">{{cite web |title=World Cup 2010 Qualifying|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/2010q.html|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation |access-date=7 July 2021}}</ref> Slovenia then won only one point in two games against the Czech Republic and lost away to Northern Ireland, and thus fell to fifth place in the group.<ref>{{cite web |title=Severni Irci spodrezali slovenska krila |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/severni-irci-spodrezali-slovenska-krila/127965 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=7 July 2021 |language=sl |date=1 April 2009}}</ref> However, the team greatly improved its form and won the last four games without conceding a single goal.<ref name="2010rsssf"/> As runners-up, Slovenia qualified for the play-offs, where they were drawn against Russia.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.dnevnik.si/1042308139/sport/nogomet/1042308139|website=[[Dnevnik (Slovenia)|Dnevnik]]|language=sl|access-date=11 June 2017|date=19 October 2009|title=Slovenija bo v dodatnih kvalifikacijah igrala z Rusijo}}</ref> The first leg was played in [[Moscow]]. The match ended in a 2–1 win for the home side,<ref>{{cite web |author1=T. G. |author2=A. V. |title=Video/foto: Gol Pečnika vrnil Slovence med žive |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/video-foto-gol-pecnika-vrnil-slovence-med-zive/216906 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=7 July 2021 |language=sl |date=14 November 2009}}</ref> and in the second leg, held in [[Maribor]], Slovenia defeated Russia 1–0 with a goal by [[Zlatko Dedić]]. Slovenia advanced to the finals due to the [[away goals rule]].<ref>{{cite web |title=World Cup misery for Hiddink as Russia slump in Slovenia |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/soccer/world-cup-misery-for-hiddink-as-russia-slump-in-slovenia-20091119-gdtt2y.html |website=The Sydney Morning Herald |access-date=7 July 2021 |language=en |date=19 November 2009}}</ref> The top scorer of the national team during the qualifying campaign was [[Milivoje Novaković]] with five goals.<ref>{{cite web |title=Milivoje Novaković, international footballer |url=https://eu-football.info/_player.php?id=23658 |website=eu-football.info}}</ref>

At the [[2010 FIFA World Cup|World Cup]], Slovenia played in [[2010 FIFA World Cup Group C|Group C]] alongside [[England national football team|England]], [[Algeria national football team|Algeria]] and the [[United States men's national soccer team|United States]]. In the opening game against Algeria, Slovenia achieved its first ever World Cup win after [[Robert Koren]] scored the only goal in the match.<ref>{{cite web |author1=R. K. |author2=A. V. |author3=Matej Rijavec |title=Foto/video: Koren odrešil Slovence velikih muk |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/fifa-sp-v-nogometu-2010/foto-video-koren-odresil-slovence-velikih-muk/232391 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=7 July 2021 |language=sl |date=13 June 2010}}</ref> In their second game against the United States, Slovenia were leading 2–0 at half-time with goals from [[Valter Birsa]] and [[Zlatan Ljubijankić]], however, [[Landon Donovan]] and [[Michael Bradley (soccer)|Michael Bradley]] scored in the second half for the United States.<ref>{{cite web |author1=S. J. |author2=M. L. |author3=T. O. |title=Foto/Video: Ob polčasu evforija, na koncu zadovoljstvo |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/fifa-sp-v-nogometu-2010/foto-video-ob-polcasu-evforija-na-koncu-zadovoljstvo/232822 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=7 July 2021 |language=sl |date=18 June 2010}}</ref> In their last match of the group stage, Slovenia lost to England 1–0 with a goal by [[Jermain Defoe]]. As the United States defeated Algeria with a late goal, Slovenia dropped to third place and were eliminated from the tournament.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/americani-razzalostili-slovenijo-136098|publisher=[[Siol]]|language=sl|access-date=11 June 2017|date=23 June 2010|first=Dario|last=Dotto|title=Američani razžalostili Slovenijo}}</ref>

[[File:Slovenia - USA at FIFA World Cup 2010, out.jpg|thumb|right|Slovenia vs. United States at the 2010 World Cup]]
After the 2010 World Cup, Slovenia achieved their highest ever position in the [[FIFA World Rankings]], as the team was ranked 15th in October 2010.<ref>{{cite web |title=Slovenija na lestvici FIFA na rekordnem 15. mestu |url=https://old.delo.si/sport/nogomet/slovenija-na-lestvici-fifa-na-rekordnem-15-mestu.html |website=[[Delo (newspaper)|Delo]] |access-date=7 July 2021 |language=sl-si |date=20 October 2010}}</ref> However, Slovenia started the [[UEFA Euro 2012 qualifying|UEFA Euro 2012 qualifiers]] with an unexpected home defeat against Northern Ireland.<ref>{{cite web |author1=R. K. |author2=To. G. |title=Šok v Ljudskem vrtu! Bridek poraz proti Severni Irski |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/sok-v-ljudskem-vrtu-bridek-poraz-proti-severni-irski/238406 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=7 July 2021 |language=sl |date=3 September 2010}}</ref> Two defeats against Italy and a home defeat against Estonia meant that Slovenia finished only in fourth place in the group, behind Italy, Estonia and [[Serbia national football team|Serbia]].<ref name="2012rsssf">{{cite web |title=European Championship 2012|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/2012e.html|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation |access-date=7 July 2021}}</ref>

===Katanec's second spell and management changes (2011–2018)===
In October 2011, the Football Association of Slovenia appointed [[Slaviša Stojanović (footballer, born 1969)|Slaviša Stojanović]] as the new manager.<ref>{{cite web |author=Sportal |title=Uradno: Slaviša Stojanović novi selektor |url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/uradno-slavisa-stojanovic-novi-selektor-43243 |publisher=[[Siol]] |access-date=8 July 2021 |language=sl |date=24 October 2011}}</ref> He led the team in only nine matches, including the first four matches of the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification|2014 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]], where Slovenia recorded one victory and three defeats.<ref name="2014rsssf">{{cite web |title=World Cup 2014 Qualifying|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/2014q.html|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation |access-date=8 July 2021}}</ref> He was sacked by the end of 2012 and was replaced by Katanec, who had previously led the team between 1998 and 2002.<ref>{{cite web |title=Slaviša Stojanović ni več selektor reprezentance Slovenije |url=https://www.nzs.si/novica/Slavisa_Stojanovic_ni_vec_selektor_reprezentance_Slovenije?id=33616&id_objekta=1 |publisher=[[Football Association of Slovenia]] |access-date=8 July 2021 |language=sl |date=9 December 2012}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last=Kos |first=Klemen |title=Srce mi bije kot prvič |url=https://www.nogomania.com/Novica/Srce-mi-bije-kot-prvic |publisher=Nogomania |access-date=8 July 2021 |language=sl |date=4 January 2013}}</ref> Under his leadership, Slovenia lost at home to [[Iceland national football team|Iceland]], before winning four consecutive games to finish third in the group, two points behind Iceland.<ref name="2014rsssf"/> 

In the [[UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying|qualifiers]] for [[UEFA Euro 2016]], Slovenia came close to directly qualifying for the tournament for the first time. The team lost a decisive game away to Switzerland.<ref name="2016rsssf">{{cite web |title=European Championship 2016|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/2016e.html|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation |access-date=8 July 2021}}</ref> Slovenia led 2–0 with less than 15 minutes remaining, but the Swiss then scored three times to win 3–2.<ref>{{cite web |author1=M. R. |title=Video/foto: Slovenija v zadnjih 15 minutah od nebes do pekla |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/novice/video-foto-slovenija-v-zadnjih-15-minutah-od-nebes-do-pekla/373332 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=8 July 2021 |language=sl |date=5 September 2015}}</ref> As the third-placed team, Slovenia advanced to the play-offs, where they were eliminated by Ukraine 3–1 on aggregate.<ref name="2016rsssf"/> The [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|qualifiers]] for the [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018 World Cup]] were similar, as Slovenia narrowly lost two decisive away matches against Slovakia and England.<ref name="2018rsssf">{{cite web |title=World Cup 2018 Qualifying|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/2018q.html|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation |access-date=8 July 2021}}</ref> After the defeat at [[Wembley Stadium]], where [[Harry Kane]] scored the only goal of the match in the 94th minute, Katanec announced his resignation as the manager.<ref>{{cite web |author1=Ž. J. |title=Katančev odstop povzročil velik preplah na Hrvaškem |url=https://www.zurnal24.si/sport/nogomet/katancev-odstop-povzrocil-velik-preplah-na-hrvaskem-298255 |website=[[Žurnal24]] |access-date=8 July 2021 |language=sl |date=7 October 2017}}</ref> In December 2017, [[Tomaž Kavčič]] became the new manager.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Viškovič |first1=Rok |title=Brez presenečenja, Tomaž Kavčič je novi selektor Slovenije |url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/brez-presenecenja-tomaz-kavcic-je-novi-selektor-slovenije-454805 |publisher=[[Siol]] |access-date=8 July 2021 |language=sl |date=4 December 2017}}</ref>

In 2018, Slovenia competed in the inaugural edition of the [[UEFA Nations League]]. In accordance with the FIFA World Rankings, the team competed in the third-tier [[2018–19 UEFA Nations League C|League C]] along with Bulgaria, Cyprus and Norway.<ref>{{cite web |last=Plestenjak |first=Rok |title=Novost Uefe, ki je poskrbela za zmedo, a tudi bogate nagrade |url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/predstavitev-lige-narodov-476913 |publisher=[[Siol]] |access-date=8 July 2021 |language=sl |date=5 September 2018}}</ref> After the poor run of results, in which Slovenia obtained only one point in the first four matches, Kavčič was sacked. He became the manager with the shortest tenure in Slovenian history, leading the team in only seven games.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Grošelj |first1=Matej |title=Mijatović odpustil Kavčiča po manj kot letu dni |url=https://www.dnevnik.si/1042843683 |website=[[Dnevnik (Slovenia)|Dnevnik]] |access-date=8 July 2021 |language=sl |date=17 October 2018}}</ref> As Slovenia finished last in the group, they were relegated to the bottom tier League D, but UEFA changed the system for the next edition and the team remained in League C.<ref>{{cite web |last=Gruden |first=Toni |title=Čeferin: "Tega nisem uredil jaz, čeprav je ugodno za Slovenijo" |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/ceferin-tega-nisem-uredil-jaz-ceprav-je-ugodno-za-slovenijo/500449 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=8 July 2021 |language=sl |date=24 September 2019}}</ref>

===Kek's second spell, Nations League promotion, and Euro 2024 (2019–2025)===
Kek was appointed as the manager for the second time in November 2018.<ref>{{cite web |title=Matjaž Kek novi selektor slovenske reprezentance |url=https://www.nzs.si/novica/Matjaz_Kek_novi_selektor_slovenske_reprezentance?id=48039|publisher=[[Football Association of Slovenia]] |access-date=8 July 2021 |language=sl |date=27 November 2018}}</ref> Slovenia failed to make a breakthrough during the [[UEFA Euro 2020 qualifying Group G|UEFA Euro 2020 qualifiers]], losing both games against Austria and obtaining only one point against [[North Macedonia national football team|North Macedonia]].<ref name="2020rsssf">{{cite web |title=European Championship 2021|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/2020e.html|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation |access-date=8 July 2021}}</ref> Despite the unexpected 2–0 home victory against Poland, Slovenia finished fourth in the group.<ref name="2020rsssf"/> However, in the [[2020–21 UEFA Nations League C]], Slovenia remained undefeated in all six games against Greece, Moldova and [[Kosovo national football team|Kosovo]]. Under the leadership of [[Captain (association football)|team captain]] [[Jan Oblak]], Slovenia held Greece to a 0–0 draw in both matches and also obtained all twelve available points against Moldova and Kosovo, thus securing first place in the group and promotion to League B for the [[2022–23 UEFA Nations League|2022–23 edition]].<ref>{{cite web |author=M. L. |title=Obramba zdržala grške napade, Slovenija v ligo B |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/obramba-zdrzala-grske-napade-slovenija-v-ligo-b/542671 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=8 July 2021 |language=sl |date=18 November 2020}}</ref> In the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group H|2022 World Cup qualifiers]], Slovenia once again failed to qualify for the main event; they started the campaign with a 1–0 home victory against Croatia with a goal by [[Sandi Lovrić]], which was Slovenia's first victory over their neighbours after failing to beat them in the previous eight official matches.<ref>{{cite web |last=Iskra |first=Uroš|title=Ibrahimović se je vrnil po petih letih in se takoj izkazal |url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/kvalifikacije-za-sp-2022-1-krog-cetrtek-548610 |publisher=[[Siol]] |access-date=15 November 2021 |language=sl |date=25 March 2021}}</ref> After two narrow defeats against Russia and a surprising defeat to Cyprus, Slovenia finished fourth in the group with four wins in ten matches, behind Croatia, Russia and Slovakia.<ref>{{cite web |title=WC Qualifiers Europe 2021/2022 – Group H |url=https://www.worldfootball.net/competition/ro112218/md0/results-and-standings/ |website=worldfootball.net |access-date=15 November 2021}}</ref>

In 2022, Slovenia made their maiden appearance in [[2022–23 UEFA Nations League B|League B]] of the Nations League, and avoided relegation after finishing third in a group with Serbia, Norway and [[Sweden men's national football team|Sweden]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Nations League B 2022/2023 – Group 4 |url=https://www.worldfootball.net/competition/ro125069/md0/results-and-standings/ |website=worldfootball.net |access-date=28 September 2022}}</ref> [[Benjamin Šeško]] scored three of Slovenia's six goals in the competition,<ref>{{cite web |title=Nations League B 2022/2023 – Top Scorer |url=https://www.worldfootball.net/competition/co4229/se42271/statistics-goals/ |website=worldfootball.net |access-date=28 September 2022 |language=en}}</ref> including a goal in the decisive 1–1 away draw to Sweden that secured a crucial point on the last matchday.<ref>{{cite web |title=Soccer-Slovenia's Sesko scores wonder goal as Sweden are relegated |url=https://www.reuters.com/article/soccer-uefanations-swe-svn-report/soccer-slovenias-sesko-scores-wonder-goal-as-sweden-are-relegated-idUKL8N30Y5G6 |website=Reuters |access-date=28 September 2022 |date=27 September 2022}}</ref>

In the [[UEFA Euro 2024]] [[UEFA Euro 2024 qualifying Group H|qualifiers]], Slovenia finally ended its major tournament drought and qualified for the Euro finals for the first time in 24 years.<ref>{{cite web |title=Slovenija po 24 letih znova na evropskem prvenstvu! |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/evropsko-prvenstvo-v-nogometu/slovenija-po-24-letih-znova-na-evropskem-prvenstvu/688910 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=21 November 2023 |language=sl |date=20 November 2023}}</ref> Šeško was once again one of the key players, scoring five goals in nine appearances,<ref>{{cite web |title=Benjamin Šeško » EURO Qualifiers 2023/2024 |url=https://www.worldfootball.net/person/pe550210/benjamin-sesko/ |website=worldfootball.net |access-date=21 November 2023 |language=en}}</ref> including a goal in the decisive home game against [[Kazakhstan national football team|Kazakhstan]] on the final matchday, which Slovenia won 2–1 in front of 16,432 spectators, a record home attendance for the Slovenia national team.<ref name="att2023">{{cite web |title=Slovenija, lahko si ponosna! V Stožicah padel nov rekord, glejte in se čudite! |url=https://ekipa.svet24.si/clanek/nogomet/reprezentancni-nogomet/655c5d7c54e48/rekord |website=Ekipa24.si |access-date=21 November 2023 |language=sl |date=21 November 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Podatek, ki je ob silnem slavju morda ostal spregledan: Stožice so zdaj reprezentančna trdnjava, padel je rekord |url=https://www.nogomania.com/Novica/Podatek-ki-je-ob-silnem-slavju-morda-ostal-spregledan-Stozice-so-zdaj-reprezentancna-trdnjava-padel-je-rekord |publisher=Nogomania |access-date=21 November 2023 |language=sl |date=21 November 2023}}</ref> The team finished the qualifiers in second place, behind only Denmark, with a record tally of 22 points from 10 games. In addition, it was also the first time that Slovenia directly qualified for a major tournament without having to play an additional play-off.<ref>{{cite web |last=Grilc |first=Nejc |title=Sanjski večer v Stožicah – Slovenija gre na evropsko prvenstvo! |url=https://www.delo.si/sport/nogomet/slovenija-kazahstan-koga-bo-kek-uvrstil-v-prvo-postavo-20-45/ |website=[[Delo (newspaper)|Delo]] |access-date=21 November 2023 |language=sl-si |date=20 November 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Benjamin Verbič z mojstrovino v Stožicah poskrbel za nov slovenski kvalifikacijski rekord: Slovenija še nikdar ni osvojila več točk! |url=https://planetnogomet.si/reprezentanca/slovenija-kvalifikacije-euro2024-rekord/ |website=Planet Nogomet |access-date=21 November 2023 |language=sl|date=21 November 2023}}</ref>

At the Euro finals in Germany, Slovenia advanced from the group stage for the first time at a major tournament after drawing all three matches in [[UEFA Euro 2024 Group C|their group]] against Denmark, Serbia and England,<ref>{{cite web |author1=K. J. |title=Slovenia advances to Euro 2024 knockout stage |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/radio-si/news/slovenia-advances-to-euro-2024-knockout-stage/712973 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=3 July 2024 |date=26 June 2024}}</ref> before being eliminated in the round of 16 on [[Penalty shoot-out (association football)|penalties]] by [[Portugal national football team|Portugal]], finishing the tournament undefeated after regular time.<ref>{{cite web |title='Po rednem delu in podaljških gremo domov neporaženi, kar je malo čudno' |url=https://www.24ur.com/sport/nogomet/euro2024/na-koncu-smo-lahko-ponosni-na-ta-uspeh-in-lahko-uzivamo-v-tem-trenutku.html |website=24ur.com |access-date=3 July 2024 |language=sl |date=2 July 2024}}</ref>

After the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group B|2026 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]], where Slovenia failed to win a single match for the first time since the 1998 World Cup qualifying campaign,<ref>{{cite web |title=Slovenci kvalifikacije za SP sklenili brez dosežene zmage |url=https://www.24ur.com/sport/nogomet/drkusic-pred-tekmo-na-svedskem.html |website=24ur.com |access-date=21 January 2026 |language=sl |date=18 November 2025}}</ref> Kek left the team after his managerial contract expired.<ref>{{cite web |title=Nogometna zveza Slovenije in Kek sta se dogovorila za konec sodelovanja |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/nogometna-zveza-slovenije-in-kek-sta-se-dogovorila-za-konec-sodelovanja/765797 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=21 January 2026 |language=sl |date=1 December 2025}}</ref> In January 2026, he was replaced by [[Boštjan Cesar]], former captain of the national team, the player with the most appearances for Slovenia, and former assistant to Kek.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Kovačevič |first1=Marko |title=Z Brda pri Kranju v Maribor in nazaj: Kako je Boštjan Cesar napredoval od pomočnika do selektorja |url=https://vecer.com/sport/z-brda-pri-kranju-v-maribor-in-nazaj-kako-je-bostjan-cesar-napredoval-od-pomocnika-do-selektorja-10403200 |website=[[Večer (Slovenia)|Večer]] |access-date=21 January 2026 |language=sl |date=20 January 2026}}</ref>

==Team image==

===Nickname and mascot===
The Slovenia national team does not have an official nickname and was the only team at the 2010 World Cup and the 2024 European Championship without one.<ref name="nickname">{{cite news|url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/fifa-sp-v-nogometu-2010/ferguson-pozabil-na-slovenijo-za-reuters-zmajceki/230613|title=Ferguson pozabil na Slovenijo, za Reuters "zmajčeki"|date=20 May 2010|access-date=17 October 2011|language=sl|publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]]|author=T. G.}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/od-olsenove-bande-do-piratske-ladje-340083|title=Od Olsenove bande do Piratske ladje|date=22 December 2009|access-date=17 October 2011|language=sl|publisher=[[Siol]]|first=Rok|last=Viškovič}}</ref><ref name="nickname24">{{cite web |last1=Plestenjak |first1=Rok |last2=Mikoletič |first2=Jaka |title=Vsi na Euru jih imajo, le Slovenija ne |url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/vzdevki-reprezentanc-euro-2024-634530 |publisher=[[Siol]] |access-date=8 June 2024 |language=sl |date=22 May 2024}}</ref> During the 2010 World Cup qualifiers, Slovenian journalists and the [[Football Association of Slovenia]] tried to choose a nickname for the team, but the process failed to gain the support of fans.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/green-dragons-vec-kot-besede-povedo-dejanja-148670|title=Green Dragons: Več kot besede povedo dejanja|date=13 January 2010|access-date=17 October 2011|language=sl|publisher=[[Siol]]|first=Rok|last=Plestenjak}}</ref> In 2010, the Slovenian web portal [[Siol]] organized a fan vote and the nickname "Kekci", a reference to the Slovenian fictional child character [[Kekec]] and the then national team manager [[Matjaž Kek]], finished in first place.<ref name="nickname24"/> However, the nickname was never officially adopted by the Football Association of Slovenia.<ref name="nickname"/> "Kekci" is still occasionally used by the Slovenian media when referring to the national team.<ref>{{cite web |author1=J. K. |title=Mladi "Kekci" izgubili proti Hrvatom |url=https://www.zurnal24.si/sport/nogomet/mladi-kekci-izgubili-proti-hrvatom-102072 |website=[[Žurnal24]] |access-date=3 September 2020 |language=sl |date=16 November 2010}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |first=Toni|last=Gruden |title="Kekci" v prvi štartni vrsti za preboj na Euro 2020 |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/novice/kekci-v-prvi-startni-vrsti-za-preboj-na-euro-2020/499296 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=3 September 2020 |language=sl |date=12 September 2019}}</ref>

During the 2010 World Cup, some foreign media articles used the nickname "Zmajčeki" ({{langx|en|Little Dragons}}) when referring to the Slovenian national team,<ref>{{cite web |title=Factbox on Slovenia |url=https://www.reuters.com/article/us-soccer-world-slovenia-factbox/factbox-on-slovenia-idUSTRE64I4NB20100519 |publisher=[[Reuters]]|access-date=3 September 2020|date=19 May 2010}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Koren: Slovenia's strength in unity |url=https://www.fifa.com/worldcup/news/koren-slovenia-strength-unity-1208051 |publisher=[[FIFA]] |access-date=3 September 2020 |date=10 May 2010}}{{dead link|date=October 2021|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref> however, this was the old nickname of Slovenian club [[NK Olimpija Ljubljana (1945–2005)|Olimpija Ljubljana]] when they competed in the [[Yugoslav football league system|Yugoslav football system]].<ref name="nickname"/> Because Olimpija had a long tradition in the former [[Yugoslav First League]], football fans and the media in the countries of the former Yugoslavia still use this nickname today when referring to the Slovenian team.<ref>{{cite web |title=Hrvatska patila i pala u Sloveniji (VIDEO) |url=https://www.mozzartsport.com/fudbal/vesti/hrvatska-patila-i-pala-u-sloveniji-video/382478 |website=mozzartsport.com |access-date=5 July 2021 |language=sr |date=24 March 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|title=Srbski strah pred glasnimi Slovenci: Slovenija je še brez zmage proti reprezentancam, ki so nastale po razpadu Jugoslavije|url=https://www.dnevnik.si/1042385687/sport/nogomet/1042385687|date=7 September 2010|access-date=17 October 2011|language=sl|website=[[Dnevnik (Slovenia)|Dnevnik]]|first=Jože|last=Okorn}}</ref>

In 2002, Slovenia received an official mascot for its appearance at the 2002 World Cup. The mascot, called Trigi, is based on a round shape representing the Earth or a ball, with a stylized depiction of [[Triglav]], the highest Slovenian mountain, on its head in a green, white and blue colour combination.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Okorn |first1=Jože |title=Trigi je maskota |url=https://www.dnevnik.si/16456 |website=[[Dnevnik (Slovenia)|Dnevnik]] |access-date=5 July 2021 |language=sl |date=24 January 2002}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Rodil se je Trigi |url=https://www.24ur.com/-214.html |website=[[24ur.com]] |access-date=5 July 2021 |language=sl |date=23 January 2002}}</ref>

===Kits and colours===
{{commons|Slovenia national football team kits}}

Until 1993,<ref>{{cite web |first=Rok|last=Plestenjak |title=Zeleno-belim dresom reprezentance odklenkalo |url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/zeleno-belim-dresom-reprezentance-odklenkalo-292150 |publisher=[[Siol]] |access-date=3 September 2020 |language=sl |date=10 December 2009}}</ref> Slovenia played its matches in white, blue and red, which are the traditional colours of the country.<ref name="autogenerated2">{{cite news|url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/se-reprezentanca-vraca-h-koreninam-45842|title=Se reprezentanca vrača h koreninam?|publisher=[[Siol]]|access-date=21 March 2016|language=sl|first2=Dario|last2=Dotto|first1=Rok|last1=Plestenjak|date=8 December 2009}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.gov.si/en/topics/national-symbols/|website=gov.si|language=en|access-date=27 March 2023|title=National symbols}}</ref> In 1993, the board of the Football Association of Slovenia decided to change the main colours to green and white, inspired by NK Olimpija from the capital city of [[Ljubljana]].<ref name="siol5">{{cite web |first=Peter|last=Prelc |title=Zeleni, beli, rdeči, modri, le enotni ne |url=https://siol.net/sportal/sportal-plus/zeleni-beli-rdeci-modri-le-enotni-ne-362985 |publisher=[[Siol]] |access-date=6 September 2020 |language=sl |date=20 September 2011}}</ref><ref name="bluekit2012">{{cite web |author1=T. G. |title=Potrjeno – novi gostujoči dres Slovenije je moder! |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/potrjeno-novi-gostujoci-dres-slovenije-je-moder/277776 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=3 September 2020 |language=sl |date=28 February 2012}}</ref>

In December 2009, the board voted to change the kit's colours to white for home matches and blue for away matches.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/nogometasi-od-leta-2012-v-novih-dresih/218909|title=Nogometaši od leta 2012 v novih dresih|trans-title=Footballers in the new kits since 2012|publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]]|date= 10 December 2009|language=sl|access-date=21 March 2016|author=A.G.}}</ref> The new colours came into effect in April 2012, when a new all-white home kit with a blue and green trim was unveiled.<ref>{{cite web |author1=D. O. |title=Vršič: Kombinacija novih dresov je lepa in nas lahko ponese tudi v Brazilijo |url=https://www.delo.si/sport/nogomet/vrsic-kombinacija-novih-dresov-je-lepa-in-nas-lahko-ponese-tudi-v-brazilijo.html |website=[[Delo (newspaper)|Delo]] |access-date=3 September 2020 |language=sl-si |date=19 April 2012}}</ref> The new away kit, introduced a couple of months earlier, was all-blue with a white and green trim.<ref name="bluekit2012"/> In 2016, the all-green version returned as an away kit, while the light blue kit became the new home kit.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/foto-po-16-tekmah-in-manj-kot-dveh-letih-ima-slovenija-nov-dres/388795|title=Foto: Po 16 tekmah in manj kot dveh letih ima Slovenija nov dres|publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]]|date=21 March 2016|language=sl|access-date=21 March 2016|author1=T.O.|author2=M. R.}}</ref> In 2022, the new Slovenian kits were selected through an online fan vote for the first time.<ref name="rtvkits2022">{{cite web |author1=M. L. |title=Kako so vam všeč novi reprezentančni dresi? |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/kako-so-vam-vsec-novi-reprezentancni-dresi/628641 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=30 May 2022 |language=sl |date=25 May 2022}}</ref> The home colours were again changed to all-white and the away colours to all-blue.<ref name="rtvkits2022"/>

For their World Cup appearances in 2002 and 2010, the Slovenian kit featured a stylized depiction of Triglav.<ref name="siol5"/> Since 2010, the depiction has been used on each new kit set.<ref name="rtvkits2022"/><ref>{{cite web |title=Slovenia Kit History |url=https://www.footballkitarchive.com/slovenia-kits/ |website=Football Kit Archive |access-date=24 March 2026}}</ref>

<div style="display:inline-table">{{Football kit box
|pattern_la =_slo92
|pattern_b =_slo92
|pattern_ra =_slo92
|leftarm = FFFFFF
|body = FFFFFF
|rightarm = FFFFFF
|shorts = 0000FF
|socks = FF0000
|title=Slovenia's first kit was white, blue and red, inspired by the Slovenian flag.<br />&nbsp;
}}</div>
<div style="display:inline-table">{{Football kit box
|leftarm = 008B00
|body = FFFFFF
|rightarm = 008B00
|shorts = 008B00
|socks = FFFFFF
|title=In the first half of the 1990s, green first appeared on Slovenia's kits.<br />&nbsp;
}}</div>
<div style="display:inline-table">{{Football kit box
|pattern_b =_slo12h
|leftarm = FFFFFF
|rightarm = FFFFFF
|shorts = FFFFFF
|socks = FFFFFF
|title=All-white home kit in 2012, with a prominent depiction of [[Triglav]].<br />&nbsp;
}}</div>

====Kit suppliers====
[[Nike, Inc.|Nike]] has been the team's kit provider since 2007.<ref name="nike">{{cite web | url = https://www.nzs.si/novica/NZS_in_Nike_se_sest_let?id=34989&id_objekta=3 | title = NZS in Nike še šest let | publisher=[[Football Association of Slovenia]]| language = sl| access-date =11 June 2017|date=12 February 2011}}</ref> Previously, the kit providers were [[Puma SE|Puma]], [[Adidas]], [[Uhlsport]], and [[Kappa (brand)|Kappa]].

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Kit provider
! Period
|-
|[[Puma SE|Puma]]<ref>{{cite web |title=Nogometna zveza Slovenije pred novimi tekmami |url=https://www.sta.si/32983/nogometna-zveza-slovenije-pred-novimi-tekmami |publisher=[[Slovenian Press Agency]] |access-date=5 September 2020 |language=sl |date=5 October 1993}}</ref>
| 1993–1996
|-
|[[Adidas]]<ref>{{cite web |title=Glasbenik krojil usodo nogometašem |url=https://www.sta.si/250967/glasbenik-krojil-usodo-nogometasem |publisher=[[Slovenian Press Agency]] |access-date=5 September 2020 |language=sl |date=20 December 1996|quote=Slovenske reprezentance bodo tako opremo Pume vsaj za štiri leta zamenjali z drugim nemškim proizvajalcem športne opreme.}}</ref>
| 1997–2001
|-
|[[Uhlsport]]<ref>{{cite web |title=NZS in Uhlsport z roko v roki |url=https://www.sta.si/633095/nzs-in-uhlsport-z-roko-v-roki|publisher=[[Slovenian Press Agency]]|access-date=3 September 2020 |language=sl |date=25 March 2002}}</ref>
| 2002–2003
|-
|[[Kappa (brand)|Kappa]]<ref>{{cite web|title=NZS dobila novega opremljevalca reprezentanc |url=https://www.sta.si/757727/nzs-dobila-novega-opremljevalca-reprezentanc|publisher=[[Slovenian Press Agency]]|access-date=3 September 2020 |language=sl |date=13 August 2003}}</ref>
| 2003–2006
|-
|[[Nike, Inc.|Nike]]<ref name="nike"/>
| 2007–present
|}

===Home stadium===
Slovenia's home matches have been held at ten venues in eight cities.<ref name="prizorisca">{{cite web |title=Vsa prizorišča |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/strani/vsa-prizorisca/3732 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=5 July 2021 |language=sl}}</ref> Since 2010, most matches have been played in Ljubljana at the [[Stožice Stadium]], with a seating capacity of 16,038.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sport-ljubljana.si/stadion-stozice//|title=Stadion Stožice|website=sport-ljubljana.si|language=sl|access-date=10 March 2016}}</ref><ref name="olimpija">{{cite web|url=https://nkolimpija.si/klub/stadion/|title=Stadion|trans-title=Stadium|language=sl|access-date=10 March 2016|publisher=[[NK Olimpija Ljubljana]]}}</ref> The final training sessions and physical preparation of the team before domestic matches are held at the National Football Centre Brdo in [[Predoslje]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Nacionalni nogometni center Brdo |url=https://radioprvi.rtvslo.si/2019/10/prvi-na-obisku-218/ |website=Radio Prvi |access-date=5 July 2021 |language=sl |date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Slovenski nogometaši začeli priprave na tri nujne malteške točke |url=https://www.sta.si/2321673/slovenski-nogometasi-zaceli-priprave-na-tri-nujne-malteske-tocke |publisher=[[Slovenian Press Agency]] |access-date=5 July 2021 |language=sl |date=7 November 2016}}</ref>

[[File:Stožice (31523159192).jpg|thumb|right|300px|[[Stožice Stadium]], the main stadium of the Slovenia national team]]

The first home stadium of Slovenia was the [[Bežigrad Stadium]], located in the [[Bežigrad District]] in Ljubljana. It was the main stadium of the national team until 2004, when [[UEFA]] banned it due to insufficient infrastructure.<ref>{{cite web |title=Za kaj vse je kriv nogomet |url=https://www.dnevnik.si/70095 |website=[[Dnevnik (Slovenia)|Dnevnik]] |access-date=5 July 2021 |language=sl |date=6 January 2004}}</ref> In the same year, the newly built [[Arena Petrol]] in [[Celje]] became the new main venue of the team for the next three years.<ref>{{cite web |title=V Celju obračun dveh evropskih presenečenj zadnjih let |url=https://www.sta.si/820494/v-celju-obracun-dveh-evropskih-presenecenj-zadnjih-let |publisher=[[Slovenian Press Agency]] |access-date=5 July 2021 |language=sl |date=30 March 2004}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Arena Petrol, Celje, football venue |url=https://eu-football.info/_venue.php?id=1119 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=5 July 2021 |language=en}}</ref> For the 2010 World Cup qualifiers, Slovenia moved to the recently renovated [[Ljudski vrt]] stadium in [[Maribor]], where the team remained undefeated in their six home matches during the qualifying campaign (five wins and a draw), consequently qualifying for the main tournament.<ref>{{cite web |title=Stadion Ljudski Vrt, Maribor, football venue |url=https://eu-football.info/_venue.php?id=535 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=5 July 2021 |language=en}}</ref> Stožice were built in 2010, and since then the venue has hosted most of the national team's home matches.<ref name="stozice-eu">{{cite web |title=Stadion Stožice, Ljubljana, football venue |url=https://eu-football.info/_venue.php?id=2068 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=5 July 2021 |language=en}}</ref> The venue also holds the record for the highest home attendance of the Slovenia national team, as 16,432 spectators gathered for the final match of the [[UEFA Euro 2024]] qualifiers against [[Kazakhstan national football team|Kazakhstan]] on 20 November 2023, which saw Slovenia qualify for the Euros.<ref name="att2023"/><ref>{{cite web |last1=Plestenjak |first1=Rok |title=Planica? Giro? Ne, Stožice! In veselica v režiji Benjaminov. |url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/porocilo-slovenija-kazahstan-620450 |publisher=[[Siol]] |access-date=21 November 2023 |language=sl |date=20 November 2023}}</ref> The attendance record was later equaled in March 2024, when Slovenia defeated [[Portugal national football team|Portugal]] in a friendly match, ending their eleven-game winning streak.<ref>{{cite web |title=Stožice več gledalcev niso mogle sprejeti! Na tribunah ponovljen sijajen obisk s tekme proti Kazahstanu |url=https://www.nogomania.com/Novica/Stozice-vec-gledalcev-niso-mogle-sprejeti-Na-tribunah-ponovljen-sijajen-obisk-s-tekme-proti-Kazahstanu |publisher=Nogomania |access-date=27 March 2024 |language=sl |date=26 March 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last1=Hindle |first1=Thomas |title=Portugal player ratings vs Slovenia: Cristiano Ronaldo's complaints fall on deaf ears in dismal defeat |url=https://www.goal.com/en-bh/lists/portugal-player-ratings-vs-slovenia-cristiano-ronaldo/blt235bc57634b5f16e |website=goal.com |access-date=27 March 2024 |date=26 March 2024}}</ref>

Other venues where Slovenia played at least one home match are the [[Bonifika Stadium]] in [[Koper]], [[Fazanerija City Stadium]] in [[Murska Sobota]], [[Domžale Sports Park]] in [[Domžale]], [[Nova Gorica Sports Park]] in [[Nova Gorica]], [[Stanko Mlakar Stadium]] in [[Kranj]], and [[ŽŠD Ljubljana Stadium]] in Ljubljana.<ref name="prizorisca"/>

===Rivalries===
Slovenia's main football rivals are its neighbours [[Croatia national football team|Croatia]]. The matchup between the two sides is known as the Neighbourhood derby ({{langx|sl|Sosedski derbi}}, {{langx|hr|Susjedski derbi}}).<ref name="derby1"/><ref>{{cite web |last1=Grošelj |first1=Matej |title=Kekovi Sloveniji vroč sosedski derbi s Hrvaško |url=https://www.dnevnik.si/1042944922 |website=[[Dnevnik (Slovenia)|Dnevnik]] |access-date=5 July 2021 |date=7 December 2020}}</ref> As of June 2026, they have faced each other thirteen times (twelve official matches and one unofficial match).<ref>{{cite web |title=Slovenia vs Croatia H2H Results |url=https://eu-football.info/_matches.php?id=181&oppo=47 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=8 June 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last1=Rijavec |first1=Matej |title=Iz TV-arhiva: Kako je Primož Gliha po hat-tricku v Splitu rezal torto |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/svetovno-prvenstvo-v-nogometu/iz-tv-arhiva-kako-je-primoz-gliha-po-hat-tricku-v-splitu-rezal-torto/574179 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=5 July 2021 |language=sl |date=24 March 2021}}</ref> One of the most notable matches between Slovenia and Croatia took place in 2003, where the two teams met in the qualifying playoffs for [[UEFA Euro 2004]]. After a 1–1 draw in the first match in [[Zagreb]], Slovenia then lost 1–0 at home and failed to qualify for its third consecutive major tournament.<ref name="derby1"/> In March 2021, Slovenia finally managed to win a game against Croatia after failing to do so in the previous nine matches, as they won 1–0 at home during the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|2022 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Gruden |first1=Toni |title=Slovenija le dočakala zmago nad Hrvaško. Veselje da, evforija pa ne. |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/svetovno-prvenstvo-v-nogometu/slovenija-le-docakala-zmago-nad-hrvasko-veselje-da-evforija-pa-ne/574228 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=5 July 2021 |language=sl |date=25 March 2021}}</ref>

==Results and fixtures==
{{Main|Slovenia national football team results (2020–present)}}

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

{{football box collapsible
|format = 1
|date = 26 September
|time = {{UTZ|15:00|2}}
|round = [[2026–27 UEFA Nations League B]]
|team1 = {{fb-rt|SVN}}
|score =

==Coaching staff==
=== Current coaching staff ===
{|
|valign="top"|
{| class="wikitable"
! Name<ref>{{cite web |title=Strokovno vodstvo |url=https://www.nzs.si/Reprezentanca/Moske_reprezentance/A_reprezentanca/Strokovno_vodstvo |publisher=[[Football Association of Slovenia]] |access-date=25 May 2026 |language=sl}}</ref>
! Role
|-
| [[Boštjan Cesar]]
| Manager
|-
| [[Bojan Jokić]]
| Coach
|-
| [[Aleksandar Radosavljević (footballer, born 1979)|Aleksandar Radosavljević]]
| Coach
|-
| [[Milivoje Novaković]]
| Coach
|-
| [[Borut Mavrič]]
| Goalkeeping coach
|-
| Srđan Prodanović
| Fitness coach
|-
| Tadej Pirtovšek
| Analyst
|}
|}

=== Manager history ===
{{multiple image
| align = right
| image1 = Pre-Iran Iraq press conference 20190115 04.jpg
| width1 = 200
| alt1 =
| caption1 =
| image2 = Matjaž Kek.jpg
| width2 = 172
| alt2 = 
| caption2 = 
| footer = Katanec ''(left)'' and Kek ''(right)'' managed Slovenia at the [[FIFA World Cup]] in 2002 and 2010, respectively.
}}
Since 1991, nine managers have been in charge of the national team.<ref name="selektorji">{{cite web |title=Vsi selektorji |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/strani/vsi-selektorji/3733 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=22 June 2021 |language=sl|trans-title=All managers}}</ref> [[Bojan Prašnikar]], [[Srečko Katanec]] and [[Matjaž Kek]] are the only three managers with more than one spell.<ref name="selektorji"/> 

[[Tomaž Kavčič]] is the manager with the shortest tenure, as he managed only seven games in 2018 before being sacked.<ref>{{cite web |author1=T. J. |author2=M. L. |title=Kavčič ni več selektor, njegov naslednik še ni znan |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/novice/kavcic-ni-vec-selektor-njegov-naslednik-se-ni-znan/469133 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=4 July 2021 |language=sl |date=17 October 2018}}</ref> He was replaced by [[Igor Benedejčič]], who became the first manager to be appointed as a [[Caretaker manager|caretaker]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Plestenjak |first1=Rok |title=Slovenijo bo vodil Igor Benedejčič |url=https://siol.net/sportal/nogomet/slovenijo-bo-vodil-igor-benedejcic-481924 |publisher=[[Siol]] |access-date=4 July 2021 |language=sl |date=2 November 2018}}</ref>

''Statistics updated as of 7 June 2026, after the match against [[Croatia national football team|Croatia]]''.

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;font-size:90%;"
|-
!Manager
!Tenure
!Played
!Won
!Drawn
!Lost
!Win %
!Major tournaments
!Ref.
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{sortname|Bojan|Prašnikar}}
|1991–1993
{{WDL|4|1|2|1}}
|
|<ref name="prasnikar-eufootball">{{cite web |title=Bojan Prašnikar, football manager |url=https://eu-football.info/_manager.php?id=497 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=22 June 2021 |language=en}}</ref>
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{sortname|Zdenko|Verdenik}}
|1994–1997
{{WDL|32|10|8|14}}
|
|<ref>{{cite web |title=Zdenko Verdenik, football manager |url=https://eu-football.info/_manager.php?id=653 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=22 June 2021 |language=en}}</ref>
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{sortname|Bojan|Prašnikar}}
|1998
{{WDL|5|1|1|3}}
|
|<ref name="prasnikar-eufootball"/>
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{sortname|Srečko|Katanec}}
|1998–2002
{{WDL|47|18|16|13}}
|[[UEFA Euro 2000|Euro 2000]] – Group stage<hr />[[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002 World Cup]] – Group stage
|<ref name="eufootball-katanec">{{cite web |title=Srečko Katanec, football manager |url=https://eu-football.info/_manager.php?id=282 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=22 June 2021 |language=en}}</ref>
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{sortname|Bojan|Prašnikar}}
|2002–2004
{{WDL|16|6|3|7}}
|
|<ref name="prasnikar-eufootball"/>
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{sortname|Branko|Oblak}}
|2004–2006
{{WDL|23|6|7|10}}
|
|<ref>{{cite web |title=Brane Oblak, football manager |url=https://eu-football.info/_manager.php?id=1201 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=22 June 2021 |language=en}}</ref>
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{sortname|Matjaž|Kek}}
|2007–2011
{{WDL|49|20|9|20}}
|[[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010 World Cup]] – Group stage
|<ref name="kek-eufootball">{{cite web |title=Matjaž Kek, football manager |url=https://eu-football.info/_manager.php?id=1202 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=22 June 2021 |language=en}}</ref>
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Slaviša Stojanović (footballer, born 1969)|Slaviša Stojanović]]
|2011–2012
{{WDL|9|2|2|5}}
|
|<ref>{{cite web |title=Slaviša Stojanović, football manager |url=https://eu-football.info/_manager.php?id=1308 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=22 June 2021 |language=en}}</ref>
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{sortname|Srečko|Katanec}}
|2013–2017
{{WDL|42|16|7|19}}
|
|<ref name="eufootball-katanec"/>
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{sortname|Tomaž|Kavčič}}
|2017–2018
{{WDL|7|1|1|5}}
|
|<ref>{{cite web |title=Tomaž Kavčič, football manager |url=https://eu-football.info/_manager.php?id=1497 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=22 June 2021 |language=en}}</ref>
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{sortname|Igor|Benedejčič}}
|2018
{{WDL|2|0|2|0}}
|
|<ref>{{cite web |title=Igor Benedejčič, football manager |url=https://eu-football.info/_manager.php?id=1518 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=22 June 2021 |language=en}}</ref>
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{sortname|Matjaž|Kek}}
|2018–2025
{{WDL|75|32|27|16}}
|[[UEFA Euro 2024|Euro 2024]] – Round of 16
|<ref name="kek-eufootball"/>
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{sortname|Boštjan|Cesar}}
|2026–present
{{WDL|4|1|1|2}}
|
|<ref>{{cite web |title=Boštjan Cesar, football manager |url=https://eu-football.info/_manager.php?id=1706 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=4 June 2026}}</ref>
|}

==Players==
{{See also|List of Slovenia international footballers}}

===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[Exhibition game#Association football|friendly]] matches against [[Cyprus national football team|Cyprus]] and [[Croatia national football team|Croatia]] on 4 and 7 June 2026, respectively.<ref>{{cite web |title=Na Cesarjevem seznamu tudi 16-letni Koren, razkrit novi dres |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/nogomet/na-cesarjevem-seznamu-tudi-16-letni-koren-razkrit-novi-dres/783226 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=25 May 2026|language=sl |date=25 May 2026}}</ref>
<br>''Caps and goals are correct as of 7 June 2026, after the match against Croatia.''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=[[Jan Oblak]]|age={{birth date and age|1993|1|7|df=y}}|caps=84|goals=0|club=[[Atlético Madrid]]|clubnat=ESP|other=[[Captain (association football)|captain]]}}
{{nat fs g player|no=12|pos=GK|name=[[Matevž Vidovšek]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|10|30|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[NK Olimpija Ljubljana|Olimpija Ljubljana]]|clubnat=SVN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=16|pos=GK|name=[[Žan-Luk Leban]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|12|15|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[NK Celje|Celje]]|clubnat=SVN}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=2|pos=DF|name=[[Žan Karničnik]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|9|18|df=y}}|caps=48|goals=2|club=[[NK Maribor|Maribor]]|clubnat=SVN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=3|pos=DF|name=[[Jošt Urbančič]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|4|12|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[NK Olimpija Ljubljana|Olimpija Ljubljana]]|clubnat=SVN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=4|pos=DF|name=[[Marcel Ratnik]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|12|23|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Al Ain FC|Al Ain]]|clubnat=UAE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=5|pos=DF|name=[[Srđan Kuzmić]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|1|16|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Viborg FF|Viborg]]|clubnat=DEN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=6|pos=DF|name=[[Jaka Bijol]]|age={{Birth date and age|1999|2|5|df=y}}|caps=73|goals=1|club=[[Leeds United F.C.|Leeds United]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=13|pos=DF|name=[[Erik Janža]]|age={{birth date and age|1993|6|21|df=y}}|caps=32|goals=3|club=[[Górnik Zabrze]]|clubnat=POL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=17|pos=DF|name=[[David Zec]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|1|5|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Holstein Kiel]]|clubnat=GER}}
{{nat fs g player|no=21|pos=DF|name=[[Vanja Drkušić]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|10|30|df=y}}|caps=30|goals=1|club=[[FC Zenit Saint Petersburg|Zenit Saint Petersburg]]|clubnat=RUS}}
{{nat fs g player|no=23|pos=DF|name=[[David Brekalo]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|12|3|df=y}}|caps=28|goals=1|club=[[Orlando City SC|Orlando City]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=7|pos=MF|name=[[Benjamin Verbič]]|age={{birth date and age|1993|11|27|df=y}}|caps=66|goals=7|club=[[Levadiakos F.C.|Levadiakos]]|clubnat=GRE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=8|pos=MF|name=[[Sandi Lovrić]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|3|28|df=y}}|caps=46|goals=5|club=[[Udinese Calcio|Udinese]]|clubnat=ITA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=10|pos=MF|name=[[Svit Sešlar]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|1|9|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[NK Celje|Celje]]|clubnat=SVN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=MF|name=[[Tamar Svetlin]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|7|30|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=1|club=[[Korona Kielce]]|clubnat=POL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=22|pos=MF|name=[[Adam Gnezda Čerin]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|7|16|df=y}}|caps=51|goals=6|club=[[Panathinaikos F.C.|Panathinaikos]]|clubnat=GRE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=24|pos=MF|name=[[Tian Nai Koren]]|age={{birth date and age|2009|7|31|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Club Brugge KV|Club Brugge]]|clubnat=BEL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=26|pos=MF|name=[[Adrian Zeljković]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|8|19|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[MTK Budapest FC|MTK Budapest]]|clubnat=HUN}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=9|pos=FW|name=[[Andraž Šporar]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|2|27|df=y}}|caps=68|goals=13|club=[[ŠK Slovan Bratislava|Slovan Bratislava]]|clubnat=SVK}}
{{nat fs g player|no=11|pos=FW|name=[[Tjaš Begić]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|6|30|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[UC Sampdoria|Sampdoria]]|clubnat=ITA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=15|pos=FW|name=[[Danijel Šturm]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|1|4|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=1|club=[[SK Slavia Prague|Slavia Prague]]|clubnat=CZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=18|pos=FW|name=[[Žan Vipotnik]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|3|18|df=y}}|caps=26|goals=5|club=[[Swansea City A.F.C.|Swansea City]]|clubnat=WAL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=19|pos=FW|name=[[Aljoša Matko]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|3|29|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Újpest FC|Újpest]]|clubnat=HUN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=25|pos=FW|name=[[Ester Sokler]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|6|4|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FK Radnički 1923|Radnički 1923]]|clubnat=SRB}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have also been called up to the Slovenia squad within the last twelve months.
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Igor Vekić]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|5|6|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Vejle Boldklub|Vejle]]|clubnat=DEN|latest=v. {{fb|MNE}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Martin Turk]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|8|21|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[G.D. Estoril Praia|Estoril]]|clubnat=POR|latest=v. {{fb|SWE}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Jure Balkovec]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|9|9|df=y}}|caps=39|goals=0|club=[[AC Omonia|Omonia]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|SWE}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Petar Stojanović (footballer)|Petar Stojanović]]|age={{birth date and age|1995|10|7|df=y}}|caps=73|goals=2|club=[[Legia Warsaw]]|clubnat=POL|latest=v. {{fb|CYP}}, 4 June 2026 <sup>INJ</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Timi Max Elšnik]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|4|29|df=y}}|caps=36|goals=2|club=[[Red Star Belgrade]]|clubnat=SRB|latest=v. {{fb|MNE}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Jon Gorenc Stanković]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|1|14|df=y}}|caps=33|goals=1|club=[[SK Sturm Graz|Sturm Graz]]|clubnat=AUT|latest=v. {{fb|MNE}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Tomi Horvat]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|3|24|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=0|club=[[Bristol City F.C.|Bristol City]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|MNE}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Dejan Petrovič]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|1|12|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[Johor Darul Ta'zim F.C.|Johor Darul Ta'zim]]|clubnat=MAS|latest=v. {{fb|SWE}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Benjamin Šeško]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|5|31|df=y}}|caps=45|goals=16|club=[[Manchester United F.C.|Manchester United]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|HUN}}, 28 March 2026 <sup>INJ</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Jan Mlakar]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|10|23|df=y}}|caps=29|goals=4|club=[[NK Maribor|Maribor]]|clubnat=SVN|latest=v. {{fb|SWE}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Nejc Gradišar]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|8|6|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=1|club=[[Al Ahly SC|Al Ahly]]|clubnat=EGY|latest=v. {{fb|SWE}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Blaž Kramer]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|6|1|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Konyaspor]]|clubnat=TUR|latest=v. {{fb|SUI}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
;Notes
*<sup>INJ</sup> = Withdrew due to injury
{{nat fs end}}

==Individual records==
{{updated|7 June 2026}}<ref name="eu-caps"/>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Slovenia.''

243 players have made at least one official appearance for the Slovenia national team.<ref name="eufootball-players">{{cite web |title=Slovenia Footballers |url=https://eu-football.info/_players.php?id=181 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=8 June 2026}}</ref> With 101 caps, [[Boštjan Cesar]] has the most appearances for Slovenia;<ref name="eu-caps">{{cite web |title=Most Slovenia Caps |url=https://eu-football.info/_players.php?id=181&data=9 |website=eu-football.info|access-date=8 June 2026}}</ref> he also had the longest career as a Slovenian international footballer with 15 years, 1 month and 15 days.<ref>{{cite web |title=Slovenia footballers by career length |url=https://eu-football.info/_players.php?id=181&data=1 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=21 June 2021}}</ref> Goalkeeper [[Jan Oblak]] started the most matches as the [[Captain (association football)|team captain]] (60),<ref>{{cite web |title=Slovenian footballers by caps as captain |url=https://eu-football.info/_players.php?id=181&data=10 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=8 June 2026}}</ref> while [[Zlatko Zahovič]] is the highest-scoring player with 35 goals.<ref name="eu-goals">{{cite web |title=Top Slovenia Goal Scorers |url=https://eu-football.info/_players.php?id=181&data=6 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=21 June 2021 |language=en}}</ref> [[Sašo Udovič]] scored the most goals in a single match, scoring five against [[Iceland national football team|Iceland]] in 1996.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Rožman |first1=Andraž |title=Nogometni rekordi: od Lewandowskega do Verbiča |url=https://www.dnevnik.si/1042721123 |website=[[Dnevnik (Slovenia)|Dnevnik]] |access-date=21 June 2021 |language=sl |date=25 September 2015}}</ref>

[[File:Cesar Slovenia 2009-11.png|thumb|170px|Defender Boštjan Cesar is Slovenia's most capped player with 101 appearances.]]
