|74
|{{#expr:13/74 round 2}}
|1998–2007
|-
|9
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Tim Matavž]]
|11
|39
|{{#expr:11/39 round 2}}
|2010–2020
|-
|rowspan="2"|10
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Primož Gliha]]
|10
|28
|{{#expr:10/28 round 2}}
|1992–1998
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Boštjan Cesar]]
|10
|101
|{{#expr:10/101 round 2}}
|2003–2018
|}

==Team records==
*Biggest victory: 7–0 vs. [[Oman national football team|Oman]], 8 February 1999<ref name="rtv-records">{{cite web|trans-title=Records|title=Rekordi |url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/strani/rekordi/3737 |publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]] |access-date=21 June 2021 |language=sl}}</ref>
*Heaviest defeat: 0–5 vs. [[France national football team|France]], 12 October 2002<ref name="rtv-records"/>
*Most consecutive victories: 4
**From 5 June 1999 against [[Latvia national football team|Latvia]] to 4 September 1999 against [[Georgia national football team|Georgia]]<ref name="rtv-records"/><ref name="eu-records">{{cite web |title=Slovenia national football team results|url=https://eu-football.info/_matches.php?id=181|website=eu-football.info|access-date=5 June 2024}}</ref>
**From 18 November 2009 against [[Russia national football team|Russia]] to 13 June 2010 against [[Algeria national football team|Algeria]]<ref name="rtv-records"/><ref name="eu-records"/>
**From 6 September 2020 against [[Moldova national football team|Moldova]] to 14 October 2020 against Moldova<ref name="eu-records"/>
**From 17 November 2022 against [[Romania national football team|Romania]] to 26 March 2023 against [[San Marino national football team|San Marino]]<ref name="eu-records"/>
**From 7 September 2023 against [[Northern Ireland national football team|Northern Ireland]] to 17 October 2023 against Northern Ireland<ref name="eu-records"/>
*Most consecutive matches without defeat: 12, from 20 November 2023 against [[Kazakhstan national football team|Kazakhstan]] to 9 September 2024 against Kazakhstan<ref name="eu-records"/>
*Most consecutive defeats: 4 (achieved on five occasions, most recently in 2014)<ref name="rtv-records"/><ref name="eu-records"/>
*Most consecutive matches without victory: 9, from 6 September 2018 against [[Bulgaria national football team|Bulgaria]] to 7 June 2019 against [[Austria national football team|Austria]]<ref name="eu-records"/>
*Most consecutive matches without scoring: 4
**From 13 October 2004 against [[Norway national football team|Norway]] to 26 March 2005 against [[Germany national football team|Germany]]<ref name="rtv-records"/><ref name="eu-records"/>
**From 5 March 2014 against Algeria to 8 September 2014 against [[Estonia national football team|Estonia]]<ref name="rtv-records"/><ref name="eu-records"/>
**From 8 September 2025 against [[Switzerland national football team|Switzerland]] to 15 November 2025 against [[Kosovo national football team|Kosovo]]<ref name="eu-records"/>
*Most consecutive matches without conceding a goal: 6, from 3 September 2020 against [[Greece national football team|Greece]] to 11 November 2020 against [[Azerbaijan national football team|Azerbaijan]]<ref name="eu-records"/>
*Most consecutive matches scoring: 14, from 19 June 2023 against [[Denmark national football team|Denmark]] to 20 June 2024 against [[Serbia national football team|Serbia]]<ref name="eu-records"/>

==Competitive record==

===FIFA World Cup===
{{main|Slovenia at the FIFA World Cup}}

|-
!colspan="9"|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan="15"|

!Round

!{{Tooltip|W|Matches won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Matches drawn, including knockout matches decided on penalty kicks}}

!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Squad

!{{Tooltip|W|Matches won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Matches drawn}}

!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}

|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]||colspan="8"|''Part of {{fb|Yugoslavia}}''||colspan="7"|''Part of {{fb|Yugoslavia}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]||colspan="8"|''Did not enter''||colspan="8"|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]||colspan="8"| ''Did not qualify''||[[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 1|5th]]||8||0||1||7||5||20
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]||Group stage||3||0||0||3||2||7||[[2002 FIFA World Cup squads#Slovenia|Squad]]||[[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 1|2nd]] ([[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)#Play-offs|PO]])||12||6||6||0||20||11
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]||colspan="8"|''Did not qualify''||[[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 5|4th]]||10||3||3||4||10||13
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]||Group stage||3||1||1||1||3||3||[[2010 FIFA World Cup squads#Slovenia|Squad]]||[[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 3|2nd]] ([[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA second round|PO]])||12||7||2||3||20||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]||colspan="8" rowspan="4"|''Did not qualify''||[[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group E|3rd]]||10||5||0||5||14||11
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]||[[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group F|4th]]||10||4||3||3||12||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]||[[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group H|4th]]||10||4||2||4||13||12
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]||[[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group B|3rd]]||6||0||4||2||3||8
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]||colspan="8" rowspan="2"|''To be determined''||colspan="7" rowspan="2"|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
!{{Tooltip|Group stage|Highest finish}}
!6!!1!!1!!4!!5!!10
!—
!{{Tooltip|2/8|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!78!!29!!21!!28!!97!!88
|}

{{notelist}}

== Head-to-head record ==
The following table shows Slovenia's all-time international record, correct as of 7 June 2026 after the match against [[Croatia national football team|Croatia]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Slovenia Head to Head against all teams |url=https://eu-football.info/_matches.php?id=181&data=1 |website=eu-football.info |access-date=8 June 2026}}</ref><ref name="rtv1991">{{cite web|url=https://www.rtvslo.si/sport/strani/vsi-izidi/3724|publisher=[[RTV Slovenija]]|access-date=26 March 2023|language=sl|title=Vsi izidi (1992–2022)|trans-title=All results (1992–2022)}}</ref>

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center; font-size: 95%;"
|-
! Team
! {{abbr|Pld|Played}}
! {{abbr|W|Won}}
! {{abbr|D|Drawn}}
! {{abbr|L|Lost}}
! {{abbr|GF|Goals for}}
! {{abbr|GA|Goals against}}
! {{abbr|GD|Goal difference}}
|-

|-

|-

== Honours ==
=== Friendly ===
* '''[[Malta International Football Tournament|Rothmans International Tournament]]'''<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesr/rothmans.html|title=Malta (Rothmans) International Tournament|access-date=31 July 2017|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesr/rothmans96.html|title=Rothmans Tournament 1996 (Malta)|access-date=31 July 2017|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation}}</ref>
**'''Winners:''' [[1994 Malta International Football Tournament|1994]]
**Runners-up: [[1996 Rothmans International Tournament|1996]]
* '''[[Cyprus International Football Tournaments|Cyprus International Tournament]]'''<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cypr-intltourn.html|title=Cyprus International Tournaments|access-date=31 July 2017|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation}}</ref>
**Runners-up: 1998, 2006
* '''Oman International Tournament'''<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tableso/omantour99.html|title=Oman International Tournament 1999|access-date=31 July 2017|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tableso/omantour00.html|title=Oman International Tournament 2000|access-date=31 July 2017|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation}}</ref>
**Runners-up: 1999, 2000
* '''[[Lunar New Year Cup|Carlsberg Cup]]'''<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesh/hk-carls02.html|title=Carlsberg Cup 2002|access-date=31 July 2017|publisher=The Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation}}</ref>
**Third place: [[2002 Lunar New Year Cup|2002]]

=== Awards ===
* [[FIFA World Rankings#Best Mover of the Year|FIFA Best Mover of the Year]]: 1999
